_comment_overview: JSON Form scaffold for POST /json-forms/forms. The struct and schema must stay in sync — struct defines the UI rendering, schema defines the data contract. Fields with customKey in struct map to property keys in schema.
_comment_struct: struct defines the form UI. Each item is a field. type controls the widget: 'string' for text/dropdown, 'number' for numeric, 'boolean' for checkbox. Dropdowns use enum/enumNames arrays. customKey maps the field to the schema property name and becomes the job variable key.

items.title: Device Name
items.description: Short hostname of the target device

items._comment: Dropdown — enum defines values, enumNames defines display labels. Each entry needs an id, label, and value. The schema.properties entry also needs matching enum/enumNames arrays (but as flat string arrays, not objects).
items.title: Environment

items._comment: Number input — optional with default value
items.title: Timeout (seconds)

items._comment: Text input — optional with default value
items.title: Comment

_comment_schema: schema defines the data contract for validation. Property keys must match customKey values from struct. required array lists mandatory field keys. enum arrays here are flat strings (not objects like in struct).

device_name.title: Device Name
device_name.description: Short hostname of the target device

_comment_uiSchema: Controls UI behavior per field. ui:placeholder sets hint text, ui:disabled disables the field, ui:widget can change the widget type.
_comment_update: To UPDATE an existing form: PUT /json-forms/forms/{id} with the body wrapped in {"options": { ...all fields above plus created, createdBy, lastUpdated, lastUpdatedBy... }}. It is a full replacement — include ALL fields.
